title: How Facebook's Algorithmic Tweaks Affected Engagement with News URLs over Time
snippet: This blog post, part of Axel Bruns' 'Snurblog', reports on Fabio Giglietto's presentation at the 2026 International Communication Association conference in Cape Town. Giglietto's research focuses on how partisan alignment, journalistic quality, and Facebook's algorithmic design influence the reach and amplification of news URLs.
description: This blog post from Axel Bruns' Snurblog reports on Fabio Giglietto's presentation at the 2026 International Communication Association conference, detailing his research on how Facebook's algorithmic design influences news engagement, partisan alignment, and journalistic quality.

title: ilrestodelcarlino.it
description: This article from ilrestodelcarlino.it discusses an investigation led by Professor Fabio Giglietto from the University of Urbino Carlo Bo, focusing on how algorithms influence online behavior, particularly in political campaigns and disinformation.

title: Secondo uno studio dell'università di Urbino l'algoritmo di Facebook ora punisce la politica ufficiale e premia gli estremisti antieuropei. Pd e FdI verso una legge bipartisan sulle piattaforme
snippet: This news article details a research study published on January 7, 2026, by the University of Urbino, authored by Professor Fabio Giglietto. The study examines the impact of Meta's algorithmic decisions on the visibility of political content on its platforms, concluding that the algorithmic suppression is less effective against extremist actors and has led to a drop in visibility for institutional politics, while increasing it for anti-European, pro-Russian, and conspiracy theory content.
description: This news article details a study by Professor Fabio Giglietto of the University of Urbino, published on January 7, 2026, examining how Meta's algorithms affect political content visibility, potentially favoring extremist narratives. It relates directly to his specialization in social media analysis and information disorder.
